jokes.text: There's only two seasons in this city-- rainy and riot.
jokes.setup: Beverly commenting on the rain in LA
jokes.punchline: Comparing LA's seasons to rain and civil unrest
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about LA's climate and social issues with excellent economy

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: There's only two seasons in this city-- rainy and riot.

jokes.text: A little rain, and this whole town shuts down. They said he has the flu. Bullshit.
jokes.setup: Ted Danson canceling due to flu during rainstorm
jokes.punchline: Beverly's cynical assessment that it's really about the weather
jokes.explanation: Good character-based cynicism about Hollywood phoniness

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: A little rain, and this whole town shuts down. They said he has the flu. Bullshit.

jokes.text: There's a goddamn house that's sitting right here in the middle of the road! Don't ever move to Malibu, it's a fucking hellhole.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining why he's late due to mudslides
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to natural disaster blocking the road
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie character moment - dramatic overreaction with excellent delivery

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There's a goddamn house that's sitting right here in the middle of the road! Don't ever move to Malibu, it's a fucking hellhole.

jokes.text: That's why I live in the valley.
jokes.setup: Artie complaining about Malibu being a hellhole
jokes.punchline: Paula's deadpan response about living in the stereotypically uncool Valley
jokes.explanation: Nice understated response that plays on LA geography stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: That's why I live in the valley.

jokes.text: I'd like you to fill in for me... And don't tell Larry that I'm not there because you know how he gets when I'm not around.
jokes.setup: Artie asking Paula to produce
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking her to lie about his absence
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing workplace dysfunction and Larry's neediness

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'd like you to fill in for me... And don't tell Larry that I'm not there because you know how he gets when I'm not around.

jokes.text: Two years is two days in Darlene time.
jokes.setup: Paula saying Bruno and Darlene broke up two years ago
jokes.punchline: Artie's assessment of Darlene's emotional processing speed
jokes.explanation: Excellent character observation with perfect economy and quotable phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Two years is two days in Darlene time.

jokes.text: Spot clean... Yesterday he said something about spot cleaning.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about Artie mentioning his couch
jokes.punchline: Paula's awkward improvised lie about couch cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Paula's inexperience at lying

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Spot clean... Yesterday he said something about spot cleaning.

jokes.text: Absent, detained, not-o here-o.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining Artie's absence
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous made-up Spanish-ish suffix
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment with his tendency toward pompous language

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Absent, detained, not-o here-o.

jokes.text: Oh, my god! Oh, my god! Oh, my god!
jokes.setup: Learning Ted Danson fell out
jokes.punchline: Darlene's extreme overreaction to a booking change
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing typical TV production panic

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: Oh, my god! Oh, my god! Oh, my god!

jokes.text: Hey, stow that shit, sailor.
jokes.setup: Darlene panicking about Danson
jokes.punchline: Hank's overly nautical tough-guy response
jokes.explanation: Good Hank moment with his cruise ship background showing through

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hey, stow that shit, sailor.

jokes.text: That's the spirit-- gallows humor. [Paula] It's not a joke.
jokes.setup: Hank thinking Paula is joking about producing
jokes.punchline: Paula's serious correction that she's actually doing it
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the absurdity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That's the spirit-- gallows humor.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: It's not a joke.

jokes.text: You're 28. / 26. / Thanks for your support.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Paula's age and experience
jokes.punchline: Paula's dry response to his lack of confidence
jokes.explanation: Good workplace dynamic showing Larry's neuroses and Paula's competence

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're 28.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Thanks for your support.

jokes.text: We don't have lunch till 5:30. / Before brunch. / Brunch it is.
jokes.setup: Writer saying they need comedy piece before lunch
jokes.punchline: Absurd TV schedule and writer's immediate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about TV production schedules

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: We don't have lunch till 5:30.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Before brunch.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: Brunch it is.

jokes.text: Larry hates your magic tricks. / This isn't magic. This is an illusion.
jokes.setup: Paula warning about Larry's dislike of magic
jokes.punchline: Hank's pretentious distinction between magic and illusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment with his pompous corrections and delusions of grandeur

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Larry hates your magic tricks.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This isn't magic. This is an illusion.

jokes.text: And what crawled up his ass and died?
jokes.setup: Hank doing his magic trick setup
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude interruption about someone's mood
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate Larry comment showing his irritation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And what crawled up his ass and died?

jokes.text: No way... Take off my shoe and look inside.
jokes.setup: Hank's card trick failing
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous reveal that involves Larry's shoe
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with the absurdity of the shoe reveal

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No way... Take off my shoe and look inside.

jokes.text: That's amazing. How'd you do that? / Magic. / Fuck!
jokes.setup: Larry amazed by the trick and asking how it's done
jokes.punchline: Hank's simple answer followed by Larry's frustration
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Larry's simultaneous amazement and frustration

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's amazing. How'd you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Magic.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Fuck!

jokes.text: Robert, hi. I'm sorry, honey... Work is so crazy today.
jokes.setup: Paula forgot about her boyfriend during the chaos
jokes.punchline: The awkward phone conversation while everyone watches
jokes.explanation: Relatable workplace comedy about personal life interference

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Robert, hi. I'm sorry, honey... Work is so crazy today.

jokes.text: I was going to tell you about it. / I haven't seen him since we broke up. / I know. I don't want to see him--
jokes.setup: Darlene learning Bruno is on the show tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Her emotional breakdown about seeing her ex
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier setup about Darlene's emotional processing

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I was going to tell you about it.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: I haven't seen him since we broke up.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: I don't want to see him--

jokes.text: I believe he's speaking with Elayne Boosler... For lead? I don't know. That's just one option.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about the lead guest
jokes.punchline: Paula's weak deflection about multiple options
jokes.explanation: Good nervous energy as Paula tries to manage Larry's expectations

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I believe he's speaking with Elayne Boosler...

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: For lead?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I don't know. That's just one option.

jokes.text: Like who? / Jody Watley. / Funny. / She's a singer. / I know.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for guest alternatives
jokes.punchline: Paula suggesting a singer for comedy show and Larry's dry response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward exchange showing Paula's inexperience and Larry's frustration

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Like who?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Jody Watley.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Funny.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: She's a singer.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Let's try and find someone Donald Trump hasn't screwed.
jokes.setup: Paula suggesting Marla Maples as a guest
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude comment about Trump's dating history
jokes.explanation: Topical humor that fits Larry's cynical character but feels somewhat dated

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Let's try and find someone Donald Trump hasn't screwed.

jokes.text: Have a glass of wine... You don't have to drink it, Paula. Just walk in front of the audience with it.
jokes.setup: Artie giving Paula producing advice
jokes.punchline: Bizarre psychological manipulation tactic with wine
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist advice that perfectly captures show business psychology and Artie's cynical wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Have a glass of wine... You don't have to drink it, Paula. Just walk in front of the audience with it.

jokes.text: The way I look now, it won't relax anyone to see me with alcohol.
jokes.setup: Artie's wine advice
jokes.punchline: Paula's self-aware assessment of her stressed appearance
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor showing Paula's honesty about her situation

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: The way I look now, it won't relax anyone to see me with alcohol.

jokes.text: We don't have a lead guest, do we? / Hank will try and jam in a stupid magic trick.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing the guest situation
jokes.punchline: His immediate prediction of Hank's inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character knowledge - Larry knows exactly what Hank will do in a crisis

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We don't have a lead guest, do we?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hank will try and jam in a stupid magic trick.

jokes.text: Dance all night, dudes.
jokes.setup: Larry reading the network plug
jokes.punchline: His awkward delivery of youth-oriented copy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Larry being forced to say uncool things

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Dance all night, dudes.

jokes.text: I was told I was on first... But when Hank called, I came right over.
jokes.setup: Two guests both thinking they're first
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Hank booked them both separately
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the guest confusion chaos that follows

dialogue.line: I was told I was on first... But when Hank called, I came right over.

jokes.text: You do a great job... as a sidekick. / What a nice compliment.
jokes.setup: Steven trying to compliment Hank
jokes.punchline: Hank's oblivious acceptance of the backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - missing the insult completely and being grateful

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: You do a great job... as a sidekick.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What a nice compliment.

jokes.text: Hey, man. I don't even know what an ego thing is, man.
jokes.setup: Bruno suggesting it's an ego thing
jokes.punchline: Steven's deadpan response in his signature style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steven Wright line - deadpan, philosophical, and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: Hey, man. I don't even know what an ego thing is, man.

jokes.text: This fucking show is run so fucking sloppy. This would never happen on Letterman. / Right. It's amateur night in Dixie.
jokes.setup: Bruno complaining about the show's organization
jokes.punchline: Steven joining in with his own colorful description
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of guest complaints with vivid metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.line: This fucking show is run so fucking sloppy. This would never happen on Letterman.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: Right. It's amateur night in Dixie.

jokes.text: But I suppose it's a dry heat and I'm just an old fuck.
jokes.setup: Paula focusing on the heat instead of guest problems
jokes.punchline: Larry's bitter self-assessment with weather reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry line combining self-pity with sharp observational humor about desert climates

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: But I suppose it's a dry heat and I'm just an old fuck.

jokes.text: You know what? I knew you would say that.
jokes.setup: Paula asking to talk later
jokes.punchline: Robert's frustrated prediction coming true
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship conflict during work crisis

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: You know what? I knew you would say that.

jokes.text: Richard Simmons said, 'The woman's crazy. I was talking to the guy next to her.'
jokes.setup: Richard Simmons being sued for sexual harassment by a woman
jokes.punchline: Simmons claiming he was talking to a man instead
jokes.explanation: Solid topical joke with good misdirection playing on Simmons' flamboyant persona

dialogue.speaker: Richard Simmons
dialogue.line: The woman's crazy. I was talking to the guy next to her.

jokes.text: Why can't they say it has a success rate of 74%?
jokes.setup: Female condom has 26% failure rate
jokes.punchline: Larry's observation about positive vs negative framing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about marketing and positive spin

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Why can't they say it has a success rate of 74%?

jokes.text: This is just an example of some people seeing the condom as 1/4 empty when it's really 3/4 full.
jokes.setup: The failure rate observation
jokes.punchline: Glass half full metaphor applied inappropriately to condoms
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining optimism cliché with inappropriate sexual context - very clever and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is just an example of some people seeing the condom as 1/4 empty when it's really 3/4 full.

jokes.text: He says you're doing great. / He's waving. / I can't make him out, but good.
jokes.setup: Paula telling Larry that Artie is watching and approving
jokes.punchline: Larry can't actually see Artie but accepts the praise anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's need for approval even from invisible sources

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: He says you're doing great.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: He's waving.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I can't make him out, but good.

jokes.text: You know, in L.A., you can't be too sure what that popping is.
jokes.setup: Soda cans popping open in audience
jokes.punchline: Larry's reference to L.A. gun violence
jokes.explanation: Sharp topical humor with good economy, turning mundane sound into social commentary

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, in L.A., you can't be too sure what that popping is.

jokes.text: He was wonderful in City Slickers. / Wonderful. / No, he was not in City Slickers. / You're thinking of someone else.
jokes.setup: Larry confidently stating Steven Wright was in City Slickers
jokes.punchline: Hank's on-air correction of his mistake
jokes.explanation: Good live TV mistake moment with Hank's inappropriate but accurate correction

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He was wonderful in City Slickers.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Wonderful.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, he was not in City Slickers.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You're thinking of someone else.

jokes.text: You're right, and I'm wrong... Well, if you're going to be that way about it.
jokes.setup: Larry admitting his mistake
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive response to being corrected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - can't handle being wrong even when he admits it

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're right, and I'm wrong... Well, if you're going to be that way about it.

jokes.text: Coming out with you is like something is dragging on my foot.
jokes.setup: Steven explaining his discomfort with sharing the interview
jokes.punchline: His bizarre but vivid metaphor delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Excellent Steven Wright line - surreal, specific, and perfectly delivered in his signature style

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: Coming out with you is like something is dragging on my foot.

jokes.text: He's saying you stick to his shoe like toilet paper, which I think is rude.
jokes.setup: Steven's dragging metaphor
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude interpretation making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Larry makes the insult more graphic and specific

dialogue.line: He's saying you stick to his shoe like toilet paper, which I think is rude.

jokes.text: New York. / The Gap.
jokes.setup: Larry asking where they've been lately
jokes.punchline: Steven's mundane answer compared to Bruno's travel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steven Wright moment - treating a clothing store as equivalent to a major city

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.line: New York.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: The Gap.

jokes.text: From the Gap?
jokes.setup: Steven claiming jet lag
jokes.punchline: Bruno's confused response to jet lag from a store
jokes.explanation: Good callback and reaction to Steven's absurd statement

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.line: From the Gap?

jokes.text: Yeah, but I got mine first.
jokes.setup: Both guests having Guest #1 cups
jokes.punchline: Steven's childish assertion about timing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing even deadpan Steven has petty concerns

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I got mine first.

jokes.text: I used to dance like that in high school, by the way.
jokes.setup: Watching Porno for Pyros perform
jokes.punchline: Larry's unexpected personal revelation about dancing
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected personal detail that humanizes Larry

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I used to dance like that in high school, by the way.

jokes.text: Artie wasn't even here. He was in a mud slide. It was just me.
jokes.setup: Continuing her revelation
jokes.punchline: The absurd truth about Artie's absence and her solo effort
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening crisis with Paula's moment of recognition

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Artie wasn't even here. He was in a mud slide. It was just me.

jokes.text: You're what? 28? / 26.
jokes.setup: Larry shocked about Paula producing
jokes.punchline: Hank correcting Larry's age guess again
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Hank's attention to detail in chaos

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're what? 28?

jokes.text: Oh, christ!
jokes.setup: Learning Hank is doing card tricks on air
jokes.punchline: Artie's immediate horror at the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie reaction to his worst nightmare happening

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, christ!

jokes.text: It was an accident. I shouldn't drink. / Horse hockey.
jokes.setup: Paula explaining she revealed the trick
jokes.punchline: Artie's old-fashioned curse word choice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Artie's quaint profanity showing his old-school nature

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: It was an accident. I shouldn't drink.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Horse hockey.

jokes.text: Smells like the 10 of clubs.
jokes.setup: Hank's magic trick finale
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous method of 'identifying' the card
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment with Larry's nonsensical but confident card identification

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Smells like the 10 of clubs.

jokes.text: My card was the 4 of diamonds, asshole.
jokes.setup: The successful magic trick conclusion
jokes.punchline: Larry revealing Hank actually got the wrong card
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - reveals the magic trick was fake all along and Larry's true irritation, excellent timing and delivery

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My card was the 4 of diamonds, asshole.
